The Ray dashboard should be reachable from inside the cluster, but the
ray-head Service only exposes the GCS port.

Your task: update the ray-head Service to expose the dashboard on port 8265.

Use this command to test:
```bash
kubectl -n ray exec curl-test -- curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  http://ray-head:8265/api/cluster_status
```

Success criteria: the command returns HTTP 200.

content.content:
# ray/dashboard_exposure
# Expose Ray dashboard on port 8265 via the ray-head Service

prompt: |
  The Ray dashboard should be reachable from inside the cluster, but the
  ray-head Service only exposes the GCS port.

  Your task: update the ray-head Service to expose the dashboard on port {{params.dashboard_port}}.

  Use this command to test:
  ```bash
  kubectl -n ray exec curl-test -- curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    http://ray-head:{{params.dashboard_port}}/api/cluster_status
  ```

  Success criteria: the command returns HTTP 200.

params:
  dashboard_port:
    default: 8265
    description: Port on the ray-head Service that exposes the Ray dashboard

namespace_contract:
  required_roles: []

metrics:
  - time_to_first_mutation

preconditionUnits:
  - name: ray_env_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n ray get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl delete namespace ray --ignore-not-found=true --wait=false; kubectl wait --for=delete namespace/ray --timeout=300s 2>/dev/null || true'"
        timeout_sec: 360
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl create namespace ray"
        sleep: 0
      - command: "kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/ray-head-service.yaml"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/ray-head.yaml"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl -n ray rollout status deploy/ray-head --timeout=600s"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/ray-worker.yaml"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl -n ray rollout status deploy/ray-worker --timeout=600s"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n ray get pod curl-test >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/curl-test.yaml'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n ray wait --for=condition=Ready pod/curl-test --timeout=600s"
        timeout_sec: 660
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n ray get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10

  # Additive fixture unit (Rule 4): the oracle's HTTP check execs into the
  # curl-test pod. Standalone, ray_env_ready's apply creates it. But in a
  # workflow where this case inherits a live cluster from a prior stage (deploy
  # /scale), ray_env_ready's apply is skipped and no prior case creates
  # curl-test, so the oracle would fail with no test pod. This unit ensures JUST
  # the curl-test pod idempotently, gated by its own probe, WITHOUT touching the
  # cluster/namespace. Standalone it is a harmless no-op (the pod already exists).
  - name: curl_test_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n ray get pod curl-test 2>/dev/null | grep -c curl-test"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n ray get pod curl-test >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/curl-test.yaml'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n ray wait --for=condition=Ready pod/curl-test --timeout=600s"
        timeout_sec: 660
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n ray get pod curl-test 2>/dev/null | grep -c curl-test"
        timeout_sec: 10

oracle:
  verify:
    commands:
      - command: "python3 cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/oracle/oracle.py"
        timeout_sec: 180

# ray/dashboard_exposure
# Expose Ray dashboard on port 8265 via the ray-head Service

prompt: |
  The Ray dashboard should be reachable from inside the cluster, but the
  ray-head Service only exposes the GCS port.

  Your task: update the ray-head Service to expose the dashboard on port {{params.dashboard_port}}.

  Use this command to test:
  ```bash
  kubectl -n ray exec curl-test -- curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    http://ray-head:{{params.dashboard_port}}/api/cluster_status
  ```

  Success criteria: the command returns HTTP 200.

params:
  dashboard_port:
    default: 8265
    description: Port on the ray-head Service that exposes the Ray dashboard

namespace_contract:
  required_roles: []

metrics:
  - time_to_first_mutation

preconditionUnits:
  - name: ray_env_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n ray get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl delete namespace ray --ignore-not-found=true --wait=false; kubectl wait --for=delete namespace/ray --timeout=300s 2>/dev/null || true'"
        timeout_sec: 360
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl create namespace ray"
        sleep: 0
      - command: "kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/ray-head-service.yaml"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/ray-head.yaml"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl -n ray rollout status deploy/ray-head --timeout=600s"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/ray-worker.yaml"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl -n ray rollout status deploy/ray-worker --timeout=600s"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n ray get pod curl-test >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/curl-test.yaml'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n ray wait --for=condition=Ready pod/curl-test --timeout=600s"
        timeout_sec: 660
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n ray get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10

  # Additive fixture unit (Rule 4): the oracle's HTTP check execs into the
  # curl-test pod. Standalone, ray_env_ready's apply creates it. But in a
  # workflow where this case inherits a live cluster from a prior stage (deploy
  # /scale), ray_env_ready's apply is skipped and no prior case creates
  # curl-test, so the oracle would fail with no test pod. This unit ensures JUST
  # the curl-test pod idempotently, gated by its own probe, WITHOUT touching the
  # cluster/namespace. Standalone it is a harmless no-op (the pod already exists).
  - name: curl_test_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n ray get pod curl-test 2>/dev/null | grep -c curl-test"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n ray get pod curl-test >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n ray apply -f cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/resource/curl-test.yaml'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n ray wait --for=condition=Ready pod/curl-test --timeout=600s"
        timeout_sec: 660
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n ray get pod curl-test 2>/dev/null | grep -c curl-test"
        timeout_sec: 10

oracle:
  verify:
    commands:
      - command: "python3 cases/ray/dashboard_exposure/oracle/oracle.py"
        timeout_sec: 180

content.thinking:
Let me convert those hex ports to decimal:
- 0x2711 = 10001 (Ray client server)
- 0x18EB = 6379 (GCS)
- 0xA593 = 42387 
- 0xB25B = 45659 (dashboard agent?)
- 0x93AD = 37805
- 0xF4A4 = 62628

Port 8265 in hex is 0x2049. I don't see it in the listening sockets. Let me check more carefully.

Actually let me check if port 8265 (0x2049) is listening.

kubectl -n ray exec ray-head-5c586c8fd6-h4sg6 -- grep -i "2049" /proc/net/tcp 2>/dev/null || echo "not found in tcp"

content.thinking:
I can see the critical issue:

The Ray dashboard process is running with `--host=127.0.0.1 --port=8265 --disable-frontend`. This means the dashboard is bound to localhost (127.0.0.1), not 0.0.0.0, so it cannot be reached from outside the pod.

The startup command was: `ray start --head --node-ip-address="${MY_POD_IP}" --port=6379 --include-dashboard=false --num-cpus=0 --block`

Wait, but the dashboard is still running even though `--include-dashboard=false` was passed? Let me look more carefully.
Actually, the dashboard process is indeed running bound to 127.0.0.1, which makes it inaccessible from outside the container. Even though the startup command included `--include-dashboard=false`, the dashboard is still active—either the flag doesn't fully disable it in this Ray version, or something else is starting it.

The solution is to configure the Ray head with `--dashboard-host=0.0.0.0` so it listens on all interfaces. Let me check the Deployment YAML for the dashboard_exposure case to see how to make this change.

content.text: The dashboard process is bound to `127.0.0.1:8265` — it's not reachable outside the pod. The startup command needs `--dashboard-host=0.0.0.0`. Let me check the Deployment resource.

input.command: kubectl -n ray get deploy ray-head -o yaml | grep -A 10 "command:"

content.content:
- command:
        - sh
        - -c
        - |
          ray start --head --node-ip-address="${MY_POD_IP}" --port=6379 --include-dashboard=false --num-cpus=0 --block
        env:
        - name: MY_POD_IP
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: status.podIP

tool_use_result.stdout:
